alerts.title: US-Iran exchange escalates in Persian Gulf; ceasefire talks stall amid repeated strikes
alerts.summary: US and Iran conducted reciprocal military strikes in Persian Gulf and around Strait of Hormuz on 6 June, with CENTCOM confirming defeat of Iranian missiles and drones per incidents feed. Multiple outlets (BBC, RFE/RL, Reuters, Guardian) report ceasefire already imperiled by exchange; Trump administration states Iran retains only 21-22% of missile stockpile. This cycle represents direct escalation despite ongoing diplomatic negotiations.

alerts.title: Iran nuclear breakout window narrows to 1-2 weeks; IAEA verification gap widens
alerts.summary: IAEA reports Iran maintains 60% U-235 enrichment with ~1-2 week breakout timeline per global_metrics nuclear headlines (Reuters, JNS, NYT, The Hill). Inspectors unable to verify enriched uranium stockpile since fighting began, creating critical information blackout. Nuclear program status unchanged despite active conflict, indicating continued advancement parallel to military operations.

alerts.title: Second Iranian attack on Gulf infrastructure; Kuwait airport hit with 1 killed, 63 wounded
alerts.summary: Iran launched attack on Kuwait airport on 6 June per incidents feed (CBS News, Guardian), killing one and injuring 63. Attack follows drone/missile exchange with US and marks expansion of Iranian strikes beyond Israel-focused targets into broader Gulf state infrastructure during ceasefire negotiations. Signals Iranian willingness to target allied civilian facilities.

alerts.title: Hezbollah formally rejects Israel-Lebanon ceasefire; Iran declares support for militant group
alerts.summary: Hezbollah rejected US-brokered Israel-Lebanon ceasefire agreement per incidents feed (NYT, CBS News, Reuters), with Iran publicly declaring support for the organization. Israeli strikes continue despite accord, killing 9+ in Lebanon and reaching Beirut outskirts. Ceasefire collapse signals diplomatic architecture failure and proxy escalation as Iran doubles down on regional allies.

alerts.title: US media sentiment deteriorates amid Trump-Netanyahu tensions and peace talk doubts
alerts.summary: US news sentiment declined to 35% positive / 56% negative per sentiment feed, with 'Trump' (60 mentions) and 'war' (11 mentions) dominating word cloud. Trump acknowledged calling Netanyahu 'crazy' and accused Israel of complicating Iran peace talks per PBS source in incidents feed. Negative sentiment spike reflects public and media skepticism toward de-escalation amid multiple simultaneous conflicts.

analysis_note: Regional ceasefire architecture is collapsing under Iranian and proxy escalation while nuclear breakout window narrows to weeks; US diplomatic leverage weakened by internal disagreements and demonstrated inability to enforce agreed-upon terms.
